title: Daniel Vollrath
content: A special education high school teacher, Daniel incorporates the Habits of Mind into his instructional practice, classroom culture, student-centered goals, and strategies for increasing productivity. Daniel is a regular blogger on the Institute, Edutopia, and currently working on a book with Solution Tree on building productive behaviors through inclusion and social-emotional learning.

title: Michele De Bellis
content: Michele's K-16 experience as special education teacher, special programs administrator and region-wide literacy director has influenced her passion for coaching to support continuous growth and that passion continues in her consulting over the last 20 years. Michele is an advocate and teacher of the Habits of Mind and views the habits as a resource and anchor to champion individual, group and organizational transformation and development.

title: Pat Mullikin
content: Pat is a former early childhood & elementary school teacher and administrator who implemented HOM in her classroom. Currently she is an Educational Consultant, Adaptive Schools Training Associate, Cognitive Coaching Training Associate, and Teacher Center Director committed to providing quality professional learning to educators.

title: Juanita Henry
content: Juanita is a retired Middle School science teacher and department chairperson who integrated Habits of Mind into her classroom and professional learning presentations.  She is a consultant who offers  many professional learning workshops including Habits of Mind and is a Training Associate for Thinking Collaborative who offers  Cognitive Coaching and Adaptive Schools Seminars.

title: Catherine Caine
content: Catherine has been a Habit of Mind practitioner at Waikiki School since 1991. Over the years, as a classroom teacher and educator, she has been dedicated to sharing her passion for what HOM brings to both her students and  community.

title: Karen-Tui Boyes
content: Karen-Tui Boyes is a champion for LifeLong Learning, a multi-award winning speaker and educator and the author of 10 books. Founder, head facilitator and CEO of Spectrum Education Limited, Sha has been leading a small (and dynamic!) team for the past 27 years, which is determined to unleash everyone’s peak performance, with a focus on ensuring teachers, students and parents have a passion for learning, understand the learning process and know how to maximize it. Karen has been working internationally alongside Art & Bena with the Habits of Mind for over 17 years.

title: Dana Lesperance
content: Dana is the Head of School at Community High School of Vermont, a NEASC accredited school that operates within Vermont’s correctional system. He has been integrating the Habits into the work and curriculum of Community High School and correctional Risk Intervention Services since 2004.

title: Denise Washington
content: Denise Washington is a Career and College Preparation Consultant, who holds a Masters in CTE Training and Development. Her focus and passion is to assist students and educators in acquiring knowledge, tools, and skills that facilitate the use of cross-cultural competencies, in hopes of impacting/increasing student success and self-efficacy in the teaching and learning environment (emphasis on Black, Brown, and Indigenous students, and Special Populations).

title: Carol Hill, EdD
content: A former high school teacher, coach, and principal, Carol Hill is currently Director of Florence County Adult Education and a professional development trainer for 16 Habits of Mind, Cognitive Coaching, and Adaptive Schools. She trusts the process of building community in order to support student and adult learners.

title: Craig Gastauer
content: A high school instructional leader and a passionate student advocate, Craig loves to collaborate with curious educators to develop co-created experiences with students in which students grow the skills and dispositions to be more thoughtful in their learning process. Craig is a regular blogger for the Institute as well as Learning Personalized.

title: Bena Kallick
content: Bena Kallick is a private consultant providing services to school districts, state departments of education, professional organizations, and public agencies throughout the United States and internationally.\r\n\r\nHer area of focus includes group dynamics, creative and critical thinking, and alternative assessment strategies in the classroom. Kallick has taught at Yale University of Organization and Management, University of Massachusetts Center for Creative and Critical Thinking, and Union Graduate School. She served on the board of Jobs for the Future.

title: Art Costa
content: Arthur L. Costa is professor emeritus of education at California State University, Sacramento. He has served as a classroom teacher, a curriculum consultant, and assistant superintendent for instruction, and the director of educational programs for the National Aeronautics and Space Administration. He has devoted his career to improving education through, more “thought-full” instruction and assessment.

title: Allison Zmuda
content: Allison Zmuda works with educators to grow ideas on how to make learning for students challenging, possible, and worthy of the attempt. Over the past 20 years, Zmuda has shared curricular, assessment, and instructional ideas, shown illustrative examples, and offered practical strategies of how to get started.

title: Henry Toi
content:
Dr. Henry Toi is the founder and Managing Director of Nurture Craft, a group of companies specialising in developing educational content in the form of curriculum and published materials. Among the books and educational materials he developed were an award winning “Phonics Zoo”, a parenting book, “Fulfilling your child’s potential”, a set of thinking books and the “Habits of S.U.C.C.E.S.S.” which is published in Singapore, India and Thailand. He also created a psychometric tool called The Habits of Mind Assessment Scales (THOMAS) that is used internationally to assess thinking dispositions of adults and students. Henry has taught classes ranging from preschool children to university undergraduate, developed a unique learning to learn programme for a university in Singapore. He has also trained more than 100 schools in Singapore and the region. Henry is an expert in the theory and practise of mind mapping, having published his thesis on mind maps and project management, and is one of only 4 Buzan Master trainers in the world.

Henry can be reached at henrytoi@artcostacentre.com.

title: Liz Locatelli
content:
\n

As a former ELA teacher, founder of an alternative program, and adjunct professor in teacher ed programs, Liz sought ways to empower learners at all levels to believe in their ability to succeed.   Over the last 12 years, she has worked in many districts throughout the state and in the DOE, helping educators to explore ways to make learning meaningful, clarify expectations and provide feedback that allows students to improve their own performance. Three years ago, she began partnering with Bena Kallick and has been amazed by the transformation she’s seen as teachers explore ways to integrate the Habits of Mind and Attributes of Personalized Learning into their instructional plans and daily practice.

\n
